## What can FAIR data do?

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="30%"}
Invasive species decision tool & alert system
[portal.pacman.obis.org](https://portal.pacman.obis.org/)
:::


::: {.column width="70%"}
![](assets/pacMAN-portal-ss.png)
:::
::::

![](assets/PacMAN-invasive-species.png)

::: {.notes}
Before I talk more about data standards, let’s look at some examples that demonstrate the power of FAIR data
This first example is work that was done through the PacMAN project (Pacific islands Marine bioinvasions Alert Network), where our team developed a decision tool & alert system for detecting invasive species. The tool uses data from OBIS AND GBIF, including dna derived detections and other types of observations. When a pest species is detected, metadata about the detection is used to determine the detection confidence and confirm its status, if it’s a real detection. This allows informed decisions to be made on how to deal with the bioinvasion
This last screenshot shows the tool and where you can see the confidence and status of detections

:::
